Firehouse.title: Firehouse
Firehouse.description: Represents a fire station or firehouse where firefighters are assigned.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Firehouse entity.

name.description: The official name of the firehouse.

location.description: The geographical location or address of the firehouse.

Rank.title: Rank
Rank.description: Defines the hierarchical rank or position of a firefighter within the department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Rank entity.

name.description: The name of the rank (e.g., "Captain", "Lieutenant", "Firefighter I").

Specialization.title: Specialization
Specialization.description: Represents a specific area of expertise or training that can be associated with training sessions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Specialization entity.

name.description: The name of the specialization (e.g., "HAZMAT", "Rescue", "First Aid").

Firefighter.title: Firefighter
Firefighter.description: Represents an individual firefighter, including their personal and assignment details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Firefighter entity.

idNumber.description: The official identification number assigned to the firefighter.

firstName.description: The first name of the firefighter.

lastName.description: The last name of the firefighter.

isActive.description: Indicates if the firefighter is currently active or inactive.

TrainingSession.title: TrainingSession
TrainingSession.description: Details about a specific training session conducted for firefighters.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TrainingSession entity.

date.description: The date when the training session took place.

startTime.description: The scheduled start time of the training session.

endTime.description: The scheduled end time of the training session.

title.description: The title or subject of the training session.

description.description: A detailed description of the training session content.

SessionParticipant.title: SessionParticipant
SessionParticipant.description: Represents the role and attendance status of a firefighter in a specific training session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SessionParticipant entity.

role.description: The role of the firefighter in the session (e.g., "Instructor", "Assistant", "Attendee").

attendanceStatus.description: The recorded attendance status for the firefighter in this session (e.g., "Present", "Absent", "Excused", "Tardy").

notes.description: Any additional notes or comments regarding the participant's attendance or role.

LeaveType.title: LeaveType
LeaveType.description: Defines the different categories or types of leave a firefighter can take.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LeaveType entity.

name.description: The name of the leave type (e.g., "Ordinary", "Illness", "Sanction").

description.description: A detailed description of the leave type.

affectsAttendanceTracking.description: Indicates whether this leave type should cause an automatic update in attendance records (e.g., 'Sanction' would not).

Leave.title: Leave
Leave.description: Records instances of a firefighter taking a specific type of leave for a defined period.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Leave entity.

startDate.description: The start date of the leave.

endDate.description: The end date of the leave.

reason.description: A detailed reason or justification for the leave.

AppUser.title: AppUser
AppUser.description: Represents a user account within the application with specific roles and permissions. This is distinct from a firefighter, though a user might also be a firefighter.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AppUser entity.

externalAuthId.description: An identifier linking this application user to an external authentication system.

email.description: The email address associated with the user account, used for identification.

firstName.description: The first name of the application user.

lastName.description: The last name of the application user.

role.description: The assigned role for the user (e.g., "Administrator", "Operator", "Assistant").

firefighterId.description: Optional reference to a Firefighter. (Relationship: AppUser 0..1:1 Firefighter). Indicates if this user account is directly tied to a firefighter's profile.

lastLogin.description: The timestamp of the user's last successful login to the application.

definition.description: Master list of fire stations. Admins can create, read, update, and delete entries. Generally readable by other roles for lookup purposes.

params.name: firehouseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Firehouse document.

definition.description: Master list of firefighter ranks. Admins can manage this static data. Generally readable by other roles for lookup.

params.name: rankId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Rank document.

definition.description: Master list of training specializations. Admins can manage this static data. Generally readable by other roles for lookup.

params.name: specializationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Specialization document.

definition.description: Master list of predefined leave types. Admins can manage this static data. Generally readable by other roles for lookup.

params.name: leaveTypeId
params.description: Unique identifier for the LeaveType document.

definition.description: Collection storing individual firefighter profiles. Admins have full CRUD access. Other roles may have read-only access depending on context.

params.name: firefighterId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Firefighter document.

definition.description: Stores application user profiles. The document ID (`appUserId`) is equivalent to `request.auth.uid`. Contains the user's `role` for database access control (DBAC) and other user-specific information. Only the user themselves can read/write their own profile, except for Admins who have full CRUD.

params.name: appUserId
params.description: Unique identifier for the AppUser document, typically matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Collection of all training sessions. Operators can create, read, update, and delete. Other roles may have read-only access.

params.name: trainingSessionId
params.description: Unique identifier for the TrainingSession document.

definition.description: Subcollection detailing participant involvement in a specific training session. Includes denormalized 'firefighterId', 'firehouseId', and 'rankId' from the Firefighter entity for authorization independence, efficient querying (QAPs), and simplified attendance reporting. Operators manage these records.

params.name: trainingSessionId
params.description: The ID of the parent TrainingSession.

params.name: sessionParticipantId
params.description: Unique identifier for the SessionParticipant document.

definition.description: Subcollection recording leave instances for a specific firefighter. Includes `recordedByUserId` for audit and a recommended `status` field (e.g., PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED) for managing approval workflows. Assistants can create/manage leaves, with Admins having oversight. Path-based ownership.

params.name: firefighterId
params.description: The ID of the parent Firefighter.

params.name: leaveId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Leave document.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure for FuegoRegistro is designed prioritizing Authorization Independence, QAPs, and DBAC. **Authorization Independence:** The `AppUser` entity is stored at `/app_users/{appUserId}` where `{appUserId}` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. The user's `role` is embedded within this document, making it the single source of truth for the authenticated user's permissions without requiring additional `get()` calls to determine their access level. This supports simple and robust security rules for DBAC. For `SessionParticipant` documents (nested under `/training_sessions/{trainingSessionId}/participants/{sessionParticipantId}`), critical authorization and filtering fields like `firefighterId`, `firehouseId`, and `rankId` are denormalized from the `Firefighter` entity. This eliminates the need for `get()` operations on the parent `Firefighter` document within security rules to check attributes related to a participant's role or assigned firehouse, thereby ensuring atomic operations and simplified rule logic. Similarly, `Leave` documents (nested under `/firefighters/{firefighterId}/leaves/{leaveId}`) inherently link to the `firefighterId` via the path, and explicitly include `recordedByUserId`, ensuring that ownership and audit trails are self-contained within each `Leave` document. **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** **Structural Segregation:** Lookup data entities such as `Firehouse`, `Rank`, `Specialization`, and `LeaveType` are stored in dedicated top-level collections (`/firehouses`, `/ranks`, etc.). This ensures a homogeneous security posture where these collections are typically read-only for most users and administratively managed, simplifying security rules. There's no mixing of publicly accessible data with private data in the same collection. **Denormalization for Queryability:** The denormalization of `firehouseId` and `rankId` into `SessionParticipant` documents is critical for enabling efficient and secure `list` queries for attendance tracking and reporting. Operators can query `/training_sessions/{trainingSessionId}/participants` and filter by `firehouseId` or `rankId` directly, without relying on security rules to filter results after an expensive data fetch. This ensures that only authorized, relevant data is returned to the client. **Path-Based Ownership:** User-owned or directly related data, such as `Leave` records, are nested under their respective `Firefighter` (`/firefighters/{firefighterId}/leaves/{leaveId}`). This makes it straightforward to write rules that allow a specific user (e.g., an Assistant or the Firefighter themselves, if allowed) to manage or view leaves tied to a particular firefighter. **Explicit State Modeling:** To address the user's issue regarding "solicitudes" (requests), it is recommended that the `Leave` entity includes a `status` field (e.g., `PENDING`, `APPROVED`, `REJECTED`). This allows for explicit state management and enables efficient queries by administrators to retrieve all `PENDING` leave requests, resolving potential visibility issues from the UI side by providing a clear, filterable state. **Overall, this structure provides a clear, predictable, and robust foundation for security rules, allowing for efficient data retrieval and simplifying debugging.
